input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets).

---

## Step 1 — Install the .NET SDK

Download the latest .NET SDK (MAUI requires .NET 8+):

```powershell
# Check if dotnet is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install via winget (easiest)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8
```

## Step 2 — Install MAUI Workloads

```powershell
# Discover available workloads
dotnet workload search maui

# Install the MAUI workload (includes Android, Windows, iOS stubs)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installation
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows target requires the Windows App SDK and WinUI. Easiest path:

```powershell
# Install VS 2022 Build Tools with required components
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Or if you want the full IDE:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community
```

Then add the required workload via the VS Installer or CLI:

```powershell
# Add .NET MAUI workload to VS (run from VS installer dir)
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat --quiet
```

## Step 4 — Set Up the Android SDK

The MAUI workload installs a basic Android SDK, but you may need to configure it:

```powershell
# Find where dotnet placed the Android SDK
dotnet android sdk info

# Discover available Android SDK packages
$androidSdkPath = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdkPath)) {
    $androidSdkPath = "$env:USERPROFILE\.android\sdk"
}

# Use sdkmanager to list available platforms & build-tools
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install a specific API level (dynamically pick the latest stable)
$available = & "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>&1
$platforms = $available | Select-String "platforms;android-\d+" |
    ForEach-Object { ($_ -split '\s+')[0] } |
    Sort-Object { [int]($_ -replace '.*android-','') } -Descending
$latestPlatform = $platforms[0]
Write-Host "Installing: $latestPlatform"

& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "$latestPlatform" "build-tools;34.0.0" "platform-tools"
```

## Step 5 — Install a Java JDK (Android build requirement)

```powershell
# MAUI/Android needs JDK 17
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Verify
java -version

# Set JAVA_HOME if not auto-set
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  "JAVA_HOME",
  "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17.*",
  "User"
)
```

## Step 6 — Android Emulator (optional but recommended)

```powershell
# Install emulator + a system image
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "emulator" "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" `
  create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" `
  --device "pixel_6"

# Verify — must have Hyper-V or HAXM enabled in BIOS for acceleration
& "$androidSdkPath\emulator\emulator.exe" -list-avds
```

## Step 7 — Accept Android Licenses

```powershell
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
# Press 'y' to accept all
```

## Step 8 — Verify Everything

```powershell
# Run the MAUI diagnostic check
dotnet workload list
dotnet new maui --list

# Create and build a test project
mkdir ~/MauiTest && cd ~/MauiTest
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android
```

## Step 9 — Enable Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Required for sideloading WinUI apps
Start-Process "ms-settings:developers"
# Toggle "Developer Mode" ON in the Settings window that opens
```

---

**Key environment variables** to confirm after setup:

```powershell
# Quick health check script
Write-Host "DOTNET:     $(dotnet --version)"
Write-Host "JAVA_HOME:  $env:JAVA_HOME"
Write-Host "ANDROID_HOME: $env:ANDROID_HOME"
dotnet workload list | Select-String maui
```

**Troubleshooting tip**: If `dotnet build -f net8.0-android` fails with SDK errors, run `dotnet workload repair` to fix corrupted workload installs.
